info.title: Poachy API Documentation
info.description: Multi-tenant POS and eCommerce Marketplace Platform API

post.description: Validate admin credentials and send OTP code to email. This is the first step of 2FA authentication.

message.example: OTP sent to your email. Please check and verify.

post.description: Verify the OTP code sent to email and complete authentication. Returns bearer token on success.
Request a new OTP code if the previous one expired or was lost. This invalidates any existing OTP.
Create a new admin or support user. Requires admin role.

role.description: User role: admin (full access) or support (limited access)

items.example: The email has already been taken.

post.description: Reset password for another admin user. Requires admin role. All existing tokens for the target admin will be revoked.

admin_id.description: ID of the admin user to reset

post.description: Logout current admin user by revoking the current access token.

get.description: Get details of the currently authenticated admin user.
Get paginated list of all tenants with their domains and business details.

post.description: Create a new tenant with domain(s). This will create the tenant database and assign domain(s).

items.example: This domain is already assigned to another tenant.

get.description: Get detailed information about a specific tenant including domains and business details.

delete.description: Delete a tenant and all associated data including database. This action is irreversible.

get.description: Search tenants by domain name or business name.

post.description: Add an additional domain to an existing tenant.

delete.description: Delete a domain. Cannot delete the last domain of a tenant.

message.example: Cannot delete the last domain. A tenant must have at least one domain.

roles.description: Array of role names assigned to the user

permissions.description: Array of permission names assigned to the user

BusinessDetailResource.title: Business Detail Resource
BusinessDetailResource.description: Business detail resource representation (to be completed later)

sanctum.description: Laravel Sanctum token authentication. Enter your token in the format: Bearer {token}
sanctum.name: Authorization
